title: Notice + action mechanism
text: Hosting providers must operate easy-to-access notice mechanisms allowing any individual or entity to flag content as illegal. Must process notices in a timely + non-arbitrary manner; provide statement of reasons for every restriction; preserve actionable notices.

title: Statement of reasons
text: Any restriction (removal, demotion, account suspension) imposed because of illegality or terms-of-service violation must come with a statement of reasons: specific content, decision, legal/contractual basis, automated decision flag, appeal route. Submitted to Commission database.

title: Trusted flaggers
text: Trusted flagger status granted by Digital Services Coordinator to entities with expertise + accuracy + independence. Notices from trusted flaggers processed with priority.

title: VLOP systemic risk assessment
text: Very Large Online Platforms (>45M EU users) must annually assess systemic risks: illegal content dissemination, fundamental rights, civic discourse, gender-based violence, public health, minors. Mitigation measures + independent audit.

title: Priority illegal content + offences against children
text: Schedule 5: priority illegal offences including terrorism, CSEA, fraud, controlling/coercive behaviour, drug supply, firearms, immigration crime. Schedule 6: priority offences for children's protection. Duty of care to remove + prevent encountering.

title: NCMEC reporting obligation
text: Electronic communication service providers must report apparent CSAM to NCMEC CyberTipline. Report contents: subscriber + payment info; geographic location info; content (image/video); copy of the communication. Failure to report = criminal liability. Preserve content 90 days.

title: COPPA — under-13 protections
text: Operators of websites + online services directed to children under 13 must: provide notice + parental consent for collection/use/disclosure of personal info; let parents review + delete; not condition participation on more PI than reasonably necessary; maintain confidentiality + security.

title: Global Internet Forum to Counter Terrorism
text: Industry consortium hash-sharing database for visually-identical terrorist content. Member platforms upload hashes (PhotoDNA / PDQ / MD5) of identified terrorist content; others can match + remove. Active for designated organizations; appeals via independent review.

title: Germany — 24-hour removal
text: German Network Enforcement Act: social networks with >2M registered DE users must remove manifestly illegal content within 24 hours of notification (or block access). Complex cases: 7 days. Transparency report semi-annually.

title: US Section 230 immunity + carveouts
text: US providers of interactive computer services generally not treated as publisher of user content. Carveouts: federal criminal law, sex trafficking (SESTA/FOSTA), IP. Voluntary good-faith moderation does not waive immunity.

title: NCII platform policy
text: Common platform policy: remove on credible report by the depicted person; do not require police report as precondition; use hashing (PhotoDNA / StopNCII) to prevent re-upload; do not host without consent of all depicted persons. UK OSA + EU DSA + state laws (e.g. US CA SB-1131) reinforce.
